title: Responsible AI Professional — Visual Library
subtitle: BC + AI Certification Program · 40 curriculum images across 4 weeks

items.title: AI vs ML vs Deep Learning
items.caption: Three nested domains, not synonyms. Deep learning sits inside machine learning sits inside artificial intelligence. Knowing where the boundaries fall is the first literacy.

items.title: GenAI vs Agentic AI
items.caption: Generative AI produces outputs you read. Agentic AI takes actions in the world. The distinction is who's in the loop after the response.

items.title: The Confidence-Accuracy Gap
items.caption: Why AI sounds right when it's wrong. The most dangerous failure isn't silence — it's a confident voice delivering a perfectly grammatical fabrication.
items.social:
AI failures aren't usually loud. They're confident-sounding sentences that are perfectly grammatical and quietly wrong. The audience nods. The compass on the podium points elsewhere. Verify before you act on the answer.

#ResponsibleAI #AILiteracy #RAP

items.title: Hallucinations vs Confabulation
items.caption: Both are made up. One is annoying; the other is dangerous. The difference is consequence, not appearance.

items.title: Global Ethics Frameworks
items.caption: UNESCO, OECD, NIST, IEEE. Four international bodies, four serious documents — partly overlapping, jointly the global ethical baseline.

items.title: Governance Approaches
items.caption: Different jurisdictions, different instruments. EU AI Act (risk-tiered law), NIST AI RMF (voluntary framework), Mitre Atlas (adversarial threat matrix).

items.title: OCAP® — Indigenous Data Sovereignty
items.caption: Ownership, Control, Access, Possession. The First Nations Information Governance Centre's principles place authority with the community, not the data requester.
items.social:
Indigenous data sovereignty isn't a checkbox. OCAP — Ownership, Control, Access, Possession — locates authority with the community, not the requester. The request is welcome at the edge of the circle; the decision belongs inside it.

#IndigenousDataSovereignty #OCAP #ResponsibleAI

items.title: CARE Principles
items.caption: Collective Benefit, Authority to Control, Responsibility, Ethics. The Global Indigenous Data Alliance's complement to FAIR data principles — not a replacement.

items.title: AI-Shaped Problems
items.caption: Some problems are pattern-matching at scale. Some are irreducibly relational. Knowing the difference is the first design skill.

items.title: Personal AI Inventory (W1 Artifact)
items.caption: The Week 1 deliverable. Document every AI system you use or oversee, with accuracy risk and governance gaps. You can't govern what you haven't named.

items.title: Types of Algorithmic Bias
items.caption: Historical, measurement, aggregation, representation. Bias enters the pipeline at distinct rupture points — you can't audit what you can't name.

items.title: Conflicting Fairness Definitions
items.caption: Demographic parity. Equal opportunity. Calibration. A mathematical impossibility theorem: you cannot satisfy all three at once.

items.title: Hiring Algorithm Bias
items.caption: Identical résumés sorted by a machine trained on a biased hiring history. The neutral surface hides the inherited pattern.
items.social:
Identical résumés. Same automated screening machine. Different outcomes — because the machine was trained on a hiring history that was already biased. The interface looks neutral. The mechanism is not.

#AIBias #FairHiring #ResponsibleAI

items.title: Privacy & Consent
items.caption: The paper consent is a sliver. The actual data flowing out is an ocean. Consent is meaningful only when scope matches reality.
items.social:
The paper consent says "I share my email." The actual data flowing out: location every 5 seconds, contacts, microphone, biometrics, third-party shares. Consent is meaningful only when its scope matches what's actually collected.

#AIPrivacy #DataConsent #ResponsibleAI

items.title: Surveillance Capitalism
items.caption: You are not the customer. Your behavior is the product. Zuboff's observation, made visible.

items.title: Differential Privacy
items.caption: Mathematical guarantee that the pattern survives while the individual disappears. The privacy budget ε is the measurable trade.

items.title: AI Training & Copyright
items.caption: Models trained on millions of works by named human creators, often without license or compensation. The legal question is unresolved; the moral one is not subtle.

items.title: AI-Generated Ownership
items.caption: When a machine produces a finished work, who holds the rights? Authorship, copyright, liability — three questions current law has not answered.

items.title: Prediction vs Description
items.caption: Correlation is not causation. AI systems predict from patterns; they do not understand mechanism. Treating one as the other is the failure mode.

items.title: Ethics Assessment (W2 Artifact)
items.caption: Week 2 deliverable. Bias risk analysis, privacy implications, copyright, recommendations. Documentation is the practice.

items.title: Deployment Readiness
items.caption: A checklist, not a hunch. Accuracy, bias audit, stakeholder review, rollback plan, monitoring, failure protocols — all six before the lever moves.

items.title: When AI Should Never Be Deployed
items.caption: Some applications cannot be made safe by tuning. Autonomous lethal decisions. Final asylum or parole rulings. Covert mass surveillance. The boundary is a category, not a slider.
items.social:
Some AI applications cannot be made safe by tuning. Autonomous lethal decisions. Final asylum or parole rulings. Covert mass surveillance. The do-not-deploy boundary is a category, not a slider.

#ResponsibleAI #AIGovernance #HardLimits

items.title: High-Stakes Domains
items.caption: Healthcare. Justice. Employment. Three sectors where AI advice carries life-altering consequences. The human still decides; the AI advises.

items.title: Human Oversight
items.caption: Meaningful, not ceremonial. The reviewer with real authority, real time, and real ability to pause the line.

items.title: Automation & Job Displacement
items.caption: Specific roles held by specific people. The transcriptionist, the agent, the picker. The transition is real; planning for the people is the responsibility.
items.social:
Job displacement isn't abstract. It's specific roles held by specific people: a transcriptionist, a customer-service agent, a warehouse picker. The transition is real. The responsibility is to plan for the people, not just the productivity.

#FutureOfWork #AI #WorkerRights

items.title: Augmentation vs Replacement
items.caption: Same craft, two deployment intents. Augmentation: the human leads, the AI extends them. Replacement: the AI works alone. The choice is design.
items.social:
Same craft, two deployment intents. Augmentation: the human leads, the AI extends them. Replacement: the AI works alone. The choice between them is design — not technology, not inevitability.

#HumanAI #Augmentation #ResponsibleAI

items.title: Worker Surveillance
items.caption: Algorithmic management replaces the human supervisor with a constant-measurement layer. The metrics hover above the floor; the manager is gone.

items.title: Environmental Cost
items.caption: One query has a cost. One training run has a much larger one. The cleanest interface in the world is fed by megawatts.

items.title: Right-Sizing Models
items.caption: Don't use a frontier LLM to sort email. The smallest adequate model is the responsible one — environmentally and economically.

items.title: Deployment Checklist (W3 Artifact)
items.caption: Week 3 deliverable. Readiness criteria, stakeholder impact, oversight, monitoring, failure response. The structured release decision.

items.title: Deepfakes & Synthetic Media
items.caption: Same gesture, same voice — one real, one synthetic. The eye cannot tell them apart at the artifact level. Provenance is the new infrastructure.
items.social:
Same gesture. Same voice. One is recorded; one is generated. At the artifact level, you cannot tell them apart. Provenance and verification are no longer optional infrastructure.

#Deepfakes #SyntheticMedia #AILiteracy

items.title: Trust Erosion
items.caption: When fakes pervade, real reports also become unbelievable. The damage is not that fakes exist — it is that the commons of shared truth frays.

items.title: AI Disclosure & Transparency
items.caption: When AI touched the work, the audience has a right to know. A standard tag, in plain sight, is the cost of doing business.

items.title: Parasocial Dynamics
items.caption: The warmth is real on one side only. Asymmetric attachment with an AI that never tires, never judges, and never calls back.

items.title: Children & Vulnerable Populations
items.caption: Time limits, escalation paths, transparent disclosure. The accommodation is design, not ban — but it is also not absence.

items.title: Creativity in the Age of Generative AI
items.caption: Infinite plausible variations recede; the chosen one glows. When machines can generate, the human contribution is taste, intent, meaning.
items.social:
When the machine can generate ten thousand plausible variations, the human contribution shifts: not production, but selection — taste, intent, meaning. The chosen one glows. The rest recede. Selection is the art.

#AICreativity #HumanElement #GenerativeAI

items.title: Agency & Autonomous Decision-Making
items.caption: The AI's recommendation is information. The decision is yours. Authority cannot be advisory.

items.title: Meaning When AI Does the Work
items.caption: When the labor that gave structure to a life is automated, the question of meaning becomes urgent. The work ends; the question remains.
items.social:
When the labor that gave a life its shape is automated, what does the day look like? The bench is still well-lit. The hands are now free. The work ends — the question of meaning does not.

#AIAndMeaning #HumanFlourishing #ResponsibleAI

items.title: Indigenous Ways of Knowing + AI
items.caption: Two epistemologies in respectful dialogue. Story and lineage; statistical pattern. Each valid in its domain; neither subsumes the other.

items.title: Ethics Impact Assessment (Capstone)
items.caption: All four weeks integrated. Technical foundations, core ethics, societal impact, human element — the comprehensive RAP capstone.
